title: Break-even analysis
learning_outcome: Calculate and interpret the break-even point using fixed costs, selling price, and variable cost per unit.
challenge_prompt: A student business sells study packs for R120 each. Each pack costs R45 to print, and the business pays R3,000 once-off for design and setup. Before receiving the formula, estimate how many packs must be sold before the business stops making a loss. Explain your reasoning.
expected_reasoning_steps: Separate fixed costs from variable costs.
Calculate contribution per unit as selling price minus variable cost.
Divide fixed costs by contribution per unit.
Interpret the result as the number of units needed to cover total fixed costs.
common_misconceptions: Dividing fixed cost by selling price instead of contribution per unit.
Treating variable cost as if it happens only once.
Giving a number without explaining what it means.
canonical_explanation: Break-even happens when total revenue equals total cost. Each unit contributes selling price minus variable cost toward fixed costs. The break-even units are fixed costs divided by contribution per unit. The result should be interpreted as the number of units needed before profit is zero.
canonical_answer: The contribution per study pack is R120 minus R45, which is R75. The fixed setup cost is R3,000. Break-even units are R3,000 divided by R75, so the business must sell 40 packs to break even.
worked_example: If fixed costs are R3,000 and each unit contributes R75 after variable cost, break-even is 3,000 / 75 = 40 units.

source.title: Sample Commerce Notes: Break-even Analysis
source.excerpt: Break-even analysis separates fixed costs from variable costs. Contribution per unit is calculated as selling price per unit minus variable cost per unit. Break-even units are fixed costs divided by contribution per unit. Using selling price alone overstates the contribution because variable cost must still be paid on each unit sold.

rubric_items.feedback_if_missing: Identify the R3,000 setup cost as the fixed cost that must be recovered.
Recognize that R45 is paid for every pack, so it reduces contribution.
Calculate contribution as selling price minus variable cost: R120 - R45 = R75.

rubric_items.criterion: Divides fixed cost by contribution and interprets units
rubric_items.feedback_if_missing: Divide R3,000 by R75 and interpret the result as packs sold.

retrieval_questions.question_text: Why does break-even use contribution per unit rather than selling price?
retrieval_questions.expected_answer: Because each unit has a variable cost, only selling price minus variable cost contributes toward fixed costs.
retrieval_questions.scoring_guidance: Look for variable cost reducing contribution.

retrieval_questions.question_text: What happens to the break-even point if printing cost increases?
retrieval_questions.expected_answer: Contribution per unit falls, so more units must be sold to break even.
retrieval_questions.scoring_guidance: The answer should connect higher variable cost to lower contribution and higher break-even units.

retrieval_questions.question_text: Transfer: A product sells for R80, has R30 variable cost, and R2,500 fixed cost. Calculate break-even units.
retrieval_questions.expected_answer: Contribution is R80 - R30 = R50, so break-even is R2,500 / R50 = 50 units.
retrieval_questions.scoring_guidance: The answer should calculate contribution first, then divide fixed cost by contribution.
